refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over agreed duration (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety alerts and operational policy constraints were respected throughout.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide the remote operator with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to achieve on-site productivity levels.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without blocking legitimate task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (productivity metrics, machine state, safety status) to enable monitoring and decision-making by Site Manager and Safety Manager.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time task planning, coupled with a low-latency teleoperation fallback and centralized safety policy enforcement engine.

DP-B: Deploy a tele-operated vehicle with operator-in-the-loop control, supported by machine learning-based task assistance and safety interlocks, with continuous telemetry streaming to a cloud-based monitoring dashboard.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid architecture: autonomous task execution for routine cycles (dig, load, dump) with remote operator override capability, integrated safety services that validate all actions against worksite policies, and a unified telemetry backbone.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid architecture with autonomous task execution for routine cycles, remote operator override capability, integrated safety services enforcing worksite policies, and unified telemetry backbone.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle autonomy (DP-C vehicle subsystem) and teleoperation (DP-C operator override) is managed via a well-defined handoff protocol and shared task state.
Coupling between safety services and vehicle control is mitigated by safety services acting as a policy enforcement layer (veto/permit) rather than direct motor control.
Coupling between telemetry and all subsystems is decoupled via a publish-subscribe interface (Telemetry interface).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids the tight coupling of pure autonomy (DP-A) and the latency/operator fatigue risks of pure teleoperation (DP-B). Hybrid approach allows productivity gains from autonomous routine cycles while preserving operator agency and safety oversight.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Routine earthmoving cycles (dig, load, dump) can be reliably automated with acceptable safety margins. Risk: If site conditions are highly variable or unpredictable, autonomous execution may fail frequently, forcing fallback to teleoperation and negating productivity gains.
Assumption: Remote operator can reliably override autonomous actions with acceptable latency (<500 ms). Risk: Network latency or operator reaction time may exceed safety thresholds in edge cases.

Assumption: Safety policy constraints can be formally specified and enforced without false positives. Risk: Overly conservative safety interlocks may block legitimate operations, reducing productivity.
Assumption: Telemetry bandwidth and latency are sufficient for real-time monitoring and decision-making. Risk: Network congestion or sensor failures may degrade situational awareness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) the boundary between autonomous and tele-operated modes (e.g., task type, site condition triggers), (2) the safety policy enforcement mechanism (e.g., rule engine, formal verification), (3) the telemetry data model and update frequency, and (4) the operator override protocol and latency budget.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the hybrid task execution logic, telemetry aggregation, and cross-system coordination to achieve the productivity KPI while maintaining safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outbound telemetry stream carrying productivity metrics (cycle time, volume moved, tonnage), vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load), safety status (alerts, policy violations), and operator actions. Consumed by Site Manager dashboard and Safety Manager monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound task commands from Site Manager or autonomous task planner. Specifies task type (dig, load, dump), target location, and constraints. Routed to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound safety alerts and policy violation notifications from Safety Services. Consumed by Safety Manager and operator interfaces for real-time awareness and intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connectivity for teleoperation commands, telemetry streaming, and safety policy updates. Connects remote operator, cloud monitoring, and on-site safety systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. It owns the hybrid task execution orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and cross-system coordination. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns the autonomous task execution and physical control subsystems, but does not own the teleoperation interface, safety policy enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. Assigning the entire FR to Vehicle would require Vehicle to depend on Teleoperation and Safety Services, creating diffusion and circular dependencies.

Teleoperation owns the remote operator interface and control latency, but does not own the autonomous task execution, safety policy enforcement, or vehicle dynamics. Assigning the entire FR to Teleoperation would require Teleoperation to own Vehicle and Safety Services, creating high diffusion.

Safety Services owns policy enforcement and safety monitoring, but does not own task execution, vehicle control, or teleoperation. Assigning the entire FR to Safety Services would require Safety Services to own Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating high diffusion and conflating safety with productivity.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of CR-NGHE-001 because it integrates the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems into a cohesive hybrid architecture. The NGHE Solution owns the task execution orchestration logic (autonomous vs. tele-operated mode selection), telemetry aggregation (productivity metrics), and cross-system coordination (safety policy enforcement, operator override handling). External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not diffuse across subsystem boundaries.
containment_risks: Risk: If the NGHE Solution module becomes a thin orchestration layer without clear ownership of key logic (e.g., mode selection, telemetry aggregation), it may become a 'god module' that depends on all subsystems without clear responsibility. Mitigation: Define explicit ownership of orchestration logic and telemetry aggregation within NGHE Solution; ensure Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are cohesive and independently testable.

Risk: If the Tasking interface is not well-specified, the NGHE Solution may receive ambiguous or conflicting task commands, leading to unpredictable behavior. Mitigation: Define a formal task schema (task type, target location, constraints) and validation rules.

Risk: If the Network Link interface is unreliable or high-latency, the teleoperation fallback may not be viable, and the hybrid architecture may degrade to pure autonomy or pure teleoperation. Mitigation: Define network latency and reliability SLAs; implement graceful degradation strategies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution module boundary to explicitly specify: (1) the task execution orchestration logic (mode selection criteria, handoff protocol), (2) the telemetry aggregation and filtering logic, (3) the cross-system coordination protocol (e.g., safety policy enforcement, operator override handling), and (4) the dependencies on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Define clear ownership of each orchestration function to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid architecture with autonomous task execution, remote operator override, and integrated safety services) directly addresses the refined FR by enabling repeatable task execution, operator control, safety compliance, and telemetry monitoring. The DP is logically sound and avoids over-specification of implementation details (e.g., specific algorithms, hardware choices).

diffusion_risks: Risk: If the NGHE Solution module does not clearly own the task execution orchestration logic, the logic may diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, creating circular dependencies and maintenance burden. Mitigation: Define explicit ownership of orchestration logic within NGHE Solution.

Risk: If the telemetry aggregation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, each subsystem may independently stream telemetry, creating redundancy and inconsistency. Mitigation: Enforce centralized telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution; define a unified telemetry data model.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module provides a natural container for the hybrid task execution architecture. The four external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not diffuse across subsystem boundaries. The Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems are cohesive and independently testable, with clear dependencies on NGHE Solution for orchestration.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable via representative operations trials: log task completion (volume, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents; compare achieved productivity against productivity_target; validate safety compliance. The NGHE Solution module owns the telemetry aggregation and monitoring logic, enabling real-time verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution owns the hybrid task execution orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and cross-system coordination. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively, with clear ownership and dependencies.

recommended_revisions: Refine the task execution orchestration logic in DP-C: specify the criteria for autonomous vs. tele-operated mode selection (e.g., task type, site condition triggers, operator availability).
Refine the safety policy enforcement mechanism in DP-C: specify the rule engine or formal verification approach for validating actions against worksite policies.
Refine the telemetry data model in DP-C: specify the productivity metrics (cycle time, volume, tonnage), update frequency, and aggregation logic.
Refine the operator override protocol in DP-C: specify the latency budget, handoff procedure, and safety interlocks for operator-initiated mode switches.

Refine the NGHE Solution module boundary: explicitly specify ownership of orchestration logic, telemetry aggregation, and cross-system coordination; define dependencies on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4), each addressing a distinct functional concern: vehicle task execution, teleoperation control, safety policy enforcement, and telemetry monitoring. The selected DP (hybrid architecture) and assigned IM (NGHE Solution) are logically sound and pass all gate reviews. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to define the detailed design parameters and implementation modules for each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable detailed design, interface specification, and verification planning.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR and selected DP are well-aligned and address the high-level productivity KPI. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner, with clear external interfaces and minimal diffusion. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are not yet atomic and require further decomposition to define the detailed design parameters and implementation modules for each subsystem. Proceeding to the next level will enable detailed design, interface specification, and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide the remote operator with sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to achieve on-site productivity levels.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without blocking legitimate task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (productivity metrics, machine state, safety status) to enable monitoring and decision-making by Site Manager and Safety Manager.

assumptions: Routine earthmoving cycles (dig, load, dump) can be reliably automated with acceptable safety margins.
Remote operator can reliably override autonomous actions with acceptable latency (<500 ms).
Safety policy constraints can be formally specified and enforced without false positives.
Telemetry bandwidth and latency are sufficient for real-time monitoring and decision-making.
The NGHE Solution module can effectively orchestrate the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems without creating circular dependencies or diffusion.
open_risks: If site conditions are highly variable or unpredictable, autonomous execution may fail frequently, forcing fallback to teleoperation and negating productivity gains.
Network latency or operator reaction time may exceed safety thresholds in edge cases, compromising the viability of the remote operator override.
Overly conservative safety interlocks may block legitimate operations, reducing productivity and failing to meet the productivity_target.

If the NGHE Solution module does not clearly own the task execution orchestration logic, the logic may diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, creating circular dependencies and maintenance burden.
If the telemetry aggregation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, each subsystem may independently stream telemetry, creating redundancy and inconsistency.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of the task execution orchestration logic (mode selection criteria, handoff protocol).
Formal specification of the safety policy enforcement mechanism (rule engine, formal verification approach).
Formal specification of the telemetry data model (productivity metrics, update frequency, aggregation logic).
Formal specification of the operator override protocol (latency budget, handoff procedure, safety interlocks).
Representative operations trials demonstrating that the hybrid architecture achieves the productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance.
Network latency and reliability measurements confirming that the teleoperation fallback is viable.
Safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) validating that the safety policy enforcement mechanism does not create false positives or false negatives.
